nts1.d: Somewhere between banging your head against a brick wall and holding your breath under water.

Artist, producer, label owner and DJ Lee Gamble bringing an oneiric bi-monthly show of current sound and music from his hallucinated musical continuum. Expect anything from Drill to Dream pop, Conceptual Sound and Jazz to bleached out rave memories..

nts2.n: Space In Between w/ Introspekt & Linda Lo (R)
nts2.d:
LA-based DJ and producer Introspekt delivers a monthly missive of UK garage, 2-step and more.
From host: _"This month Introspekt is joined by LA-based Linda Lo for a special dubtastic b2b 2-Step throw down. Expect lots of classic headsy cuts as well as contemporary productions from Linda Lo and friends." _

Joyful global club sounds with NYC via London party starter The Large
From the host: "In the 100bpm bag, slow / sexy / synthy !!"

subtle.n: The Watercourse Way w/ Jamie Nu
Stepfwd w/ Dub Hustle & Death Trap

subtle.d: Hailing from Belfast but making moves in Nottingham, Nikki O is a DJ and radio presenter whose sets blend 4/4 rhythms, breakbeats, and the bassier side of techno.

DJ & Producer, B-Say has been a Subtle resident DJ for over 3 years.

His radio shows tend to focus on the 140 bass music scene and aims to showcase unreleased/forthcoming music from upcoming artists, as well as premiering his own unreleased material. 

More recently B-Say has ventured out from solely DJ'ing Dubstep, with his bi-monthly 'Dub & Steppers Show' which he uses to premier his own productions.

Slick is a London-based Producer and DJ combining the sounds of Grime and Drill.
Expect sets to span from sparse wave and weightless to classic NY and UK Drill, with everything in between
J:KENZO is a producer and record label owner (Artikal Music & Lion Charge) from the UK. Known for his minimalistic dark tribal sound in the both the dubstep and drum and bass scenes since 2006.

dublab.d: “Dying Songs focuses on melancholic electronic music but isn’t limited to that. Sometimes it gets dark, sometimes I try to mix techno awkwardly, sometimes i get nostalgic… I like stuff that’s dreamy, ghostly, pretty and sad. I’ll try out new productions of my own, as well. I’m keeping it pretty open and we’ll see how it evolves.” – Jimmy
